title: Troubleshooting/error-message docs for a high-traffic tool
context: High-traffic cancer research tools often have issues trackers full of repeated 'how do I fix error X' questions that a well-written troubleshooting or error-message documentation section would resolve. This cuts the maintainer's support burden and helps new users get unstuck faster. riskTier is low: troubleshooting docs are developer/researcher-facing, do not include biological claims, and do not require external data. The target tool must have a PASS gate artifact.
objective: Author and contribute merged troubleshooting or error-message documentation for a high-traffic, gate-passed cancer research tool, addressing the most common user-facing errors documented in the tool's issue tracker.
acceptanceCriteria: Target tool has a PASS gate artifact confirming OSI-permissive or copyleft license and acceptable contribution policy.
Issue-first alignment confirmed with the maintainer; the specific error messages or FAQ entries are confirmed as wanted.
Troubleshooting docs authored following the templates (template-003); each entry links to the relevant issue or code path for context.
No behavioral changes; no biological/clinical claims; no patient data.
License + Data-provenance reviewer sign-off and Technical reviewer sign-off obtained.
Contribution merged upstream; outcome artifact committed at outcomes/onco-research-software-docs-trouble-024.json with merge-commit URL.
resources: Gate artifacts from triage-009 or a new gate assessment for the target tool
Target tool's issue tracker (common error patterns)
output: Troubleshooting/error-message documentation merged upstream for a high-traffic gate-passed cancer research tool, with outcome artifact recorded — cutting maintainer support burden and helping new users get unstuck.
